content:
Next Steps:
• Choose a fourth inspiration that fits your vision.
• Write and revise your logline five different ways.
• Experiment with structuring your story in three different ways.

Would you like this formatted as a workbook with exercises? Let me know, and I can make it into a polished PDF for easy reference.

SECTION 1: STUDYING YOUR INSPIRATIONS (continued)

Your main inspirations:
• Whiplash (2014) – A film about artistic obsession, mentorship, and the cost of greatness.
• Real (Takehiko Inoue) – A manga about wheelchair basketball, personal struggle, and self-worth.
• Vagabond (Takehiko Inoue) – A samurai epic exploring the philosophy of violence and growth.

Since you’re working in the graphic novel format, the fourth inspiration should match the tone, depth, and artistic ambition of these works. Here are three strong contenders:

Three Additional Graphic Novels That Fit the Vibe
1. A Silent Voice (Yoshitoki Ōima) – A deeply emotional manga about redemption, disability, and the struggle for forgiveness. Like Real, it explores physical and emotional struggles with a focus on inner conflict and personal growth.
